objective: Implement the first local source-repo startup slice under issue #615 by surfacing lane-state at fresh-session start and failing closed when unique claimed-scope discovery cannot be resolved for an execution-capable session.
scope_boundary: Owns the local startup/helper surfacing path and fail-closed unique-scope discovery only.

Keeps #607 as the planning-authority dependency, #612 as the schema/CI degraded-fallback enforcement lane, #614 as the downstream consumer verifier lane, and leaves broader pre-hook write blocking for a later child under #615.
Starts by producing the issue-local bootstrap packet and validation evidence in this isolated worktree.
out_of_scope: Planning-authority contract rewrites that belong to issue #607.
Execution-scope schema or CI validator changes that belong to issue #612.
Downstream hldpro-sim consumer drift verifier work that belongs to issue #614.
Any claim of repo-wide fresh-session impossibility.
research_summary: The pending mainline action before the Claude wrapper failure was to leave issue #610 honestly blocked and move into the first implementation child under issue #615. Specialist review then narrowed the smallest valid first slice further: startup-only lane-state surfacing plus fail-closed unique-scope discovery on the local canonical startup/helper path. Live source inspection confirms the first-slice surfaces are .claude/settings.json, hooks/pre-session-context.sh, and scripts/overlord/check_execution_environment.py with focused startup tests. The lane therefore begins with an issue-local implementation packet before startup-surface changes proceed.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local implementation packet and execution-scope boundaries before startup/helper changes.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, handoff, review packet, review placeholders, and validation artifact.
Restate the narrowed startup-only write surfaces confirmed by specialist review.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet is validated.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names the exact startup/helper owned surfaces.
The packet keeps #607, #612, and #614 as explicit external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only the issue-local packet files during bootstrap.

sprints.name: Startup Implementation
sprints.goal: Surface active lane-state at startup and fail closed when unique claimed-scope discovery cannot be resolved for the current execution-capable session.
tasks: Promote issue #617 into an implementation-ready execution scope bounded to the startup/helper surfaces and focused tests.
Update .claude/settings.json so UserPromptSubmit invokes the repo-local startup hook path instead of a hardcoded home-root governance checkout path.
Extend scripts/overlord/check_execution_environment.py with startup discovery and summary mode for unique claimed-scope resolution, lane-claim enforcement, and compact output.
Update hooks/pre-session-context.sh to emit the canonical bootstrap contract note and then append PASS or BLOCKED startup execution-context output from the startup discovery mode.
Co-stage docs/PROGRESS.md, docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md, and the relevant docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md entry required for governance-surface implementation changes.
Create the issue-local Stage 6 closeout artifact for the bounded startup/helper implementation slice.
Add focused tests and validation artifacts for startup success and expected-fail transcripts only.
acceptance_criteria: Startup hook output includes a deterministic PASS or BLOCKED execution-context summary after the bootstrap contract note.
The startup summary names the resolved scope path, issue number, execution_mode, next_role or next_execution_step, and required specialist signals when a unique claimed scope is found.
Startup blocks clearly when no unique claimed scope is resolved or the resolved scope fails branch or lane-claim validation.
The bounded implementation co-stages docs/PROGRESS.md, docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md, and the relevant docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md entry.
The bounded implementation includes a valid Stage 6 closeout artifact.
No broader pre-hook write-blocking surfaces are changed.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the narrowest valid first implementation slice under issue #615.
specialist_reviews.summary: The smallest valid first child is startup-only lane-state surfacing and fail-closed unique-scope discovery on the local canonical startup/helper path. Broader pre-hook write-blocking remains a later child under #615.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the startup child keeps #607, #612, and #614 explicit as separate lanes and carries the startup proof bullets from issue #615.
specialist_reviews.summary: The startup child may own startup/helper fail-closed surfacing and unique-scope discovery on the local canonical startup path, but it must keep #607 as the planning-authority dependency, #612 as the schema/CI degraded-fallback lane, and #614 as the downstream verifier lane.

alternate_model_review.summary: Claude alternate-family review accepted the bounded startup/helper implementation slice with no blocking findings and one residual design-only follow-up about planning-only startup warnings.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #617 is authorized for the bounded startup/helper implementation slice on the repo-local startup hook, execution-environment helper, and focused startup tests only.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implementation is complete for the bounded startup/helper slice. Use the implementation-ready handoff and validation artifacts for closeout, audit, and issue-state reconciliation only.
blocked_on: Issue #607 remains the planning-authority dependency for startup and fresh-session proof.
Issue #612 remains the schema/CI degraded-fallback enforcement lane.
Issue #614 remains the downstream hldpro-sim verifier/drift-gate lane.
Broader pre-hook write-blocking remains a later child under issue #615.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Startup hook output appends a deterministic PASS or BLOCKED execution-context summary after the canonical bootstrap contract note.

The startup summary resolves the unique claimed scope for the current issue branch and surfaces scope path, issue number, execution_mode, next_role or next_execution_step, and required specialist signals.
Startup blocks clearly when no unique claimed scope is resolved for the current issue branch, including missing-scope and conflicting-scope conditions.
Issue #617 remains bounded to startup/helper surfacing only and does not edit broader pre-hook write-blocking or sibling-lane contracts.

material_deviation_rules: Any change to #607, #612, or #614 owned surfaces from issue #617 is a material deviation.
Any claim that issue #617 closes repo-wide fresh-session mis-routing is a material deviation.
Any broader pre-hook write-blocking implementation in issue #617 is a material deviation.
Any hook/helper implementation before the issue-local packet is validated is a material deviation.
